title: Fix media loading for authenticated admin users

requirements.text: Modify getMediaAssets backend function to allow access for any authenticated user, removing strict principal validation that blocks media listing

quotes: Ensure getMediaAssets is accessible for authenticated admin users
Do not block media listing with strict principal validation

acceptanceCriteria: getMediaAssets returns media assets for authenticated users without principal validation
Admin users can view media list immediately after login
Function does not throw authorization errors for valid authenticated sessions

requirements.text: Ensure deleteMediaAsset retains role-based authorization checking for 'admin' role only, keeping existing admin validation logic

quotes: Keep admin validation only for deleteMediaAsset

acceptanceCriteria: deleteMediaAsset continues to validate admin role before allowing deletion
Non-admin users cannot delete media assets
Authorization logic remains unchanged for delete operations

requirements.text: Verify media upload logic remains unmodified and continues to function correctly
acceptanceCriteria: Upload authorization and validation logic is preserved

requirements.text: Test and verify that media list loads correctly on the admin media manager page after user authentication and login

quotes: Ensure media list loads correctly after login

acceptanceCriteria: Media assets display in the admin media manager immediately after login
No authentication errors appear when loading media list
MediaAssetGrid component successfully renders uploaded assets

constraints: Do not modify the upload logic or uploadMediaAsset function
Maintain role-based authorization for deleteMediaAsset using admin role validation
Remove principal validation only from getMediaAssets, not from delete operations
nonGoals: Modifying authorization logic for other admin operations
Altering the frontend media display components
